subject: Geography
title: The Arctic Ocean
description: A fast breakdown of The Arctic Ocean for builders.

hook.text: The Arctic Ocean is the smallest and shallowest ocean, yet it's covered by ice most of the year.

buildup.text: Its ice cap shrinks in summer and regrows in winter, pulsing like a frozen heartbeat.

discovery.text: Beneath the ice, the ocean teems with plankton that feed whales, seals, and Arctic fish.

twist.text: Submarines have traveled under the Arctic ice cap—the USS Nautilus crossed it in 1958.

climax.text: The Arctic Ocean connects the Atlantic and Pacific through passages opening as ice retreats.

punchline.text: The world's smallest ocean may become its busiest shortcut.

quiz.question: What was the USS Nautilus the first to do in 1958?
options: Travel under the Arctic ice cap
